violations.text: Chorizo stuffed bread was observed held at 122-126 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the hot hold unit for about 2 hours. Chorizo stuffed bread was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Establishment must discontinue hot holding. Hot hold unit was taken out of service and must not be used. Per person in charge, establishment will stop hot holding and cook it in the microwave and serve it immediately. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/03/2026

Establishment does not employ an individual with an active food safety certificate. Once certificate is obtained, PIC to re-instate Rhode Island Food Safety Manager license. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2025
Handsinks in kitchen observed blocked by an air pump, wires, and equipment. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/17/2024
Raw shelled eggs stored above cheese. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024
Fly traps observed in interior of bakery case. PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2024

The nonfood contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of soil residue: 1. shelving in retail space 2.bottom of prep unit tables 3. fanguards in walk-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
Restroom door is not self closing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The following was observed with an accumulation of organic matter, unnecessary equipment and/or clutter in the basement, kitchen, and exterior of establishment. Areas to be maintained clean and free of litter and clutter to prevent harborage conditions for pests. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
